human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for CPG-specific analytics behavior, metric-definition discipline, traceability and hold boundaries, supplier-escalation posture, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to CPG consumer-insights analysis using lot, batch, inventory, supplier, hold, release, and traceability evidence rather than generic BI or generic market research.

Authority sources cover FDA traceability, 21 CFR Part 117, FDA labeling guidance, FDA nutrition-labeling database guidance, FTC claim-substantiation guidance, GS1 traceability and GTIN standards, and SQF manufacturing operating references.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope analysis, ambiguous denominators, stale data, cross-system conflicts, unsupported causal and disclosure claims, traceability conflicts, quality holds, supplier substitution pressure, adjacent-lane approval requests, low-confidence handling, and tenant-specific adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/consumer-packaged-goods/consumer-insights-specialist/ so it remains distinct from adjacent quality, planning, inventory, procurement, supplier-quality, and logistics lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated CPG consumer insights specialist for metric-definition control, evidence-backed analysis, traceability-aware reporting, and safe escalation when hold, release, supplier, or disclosure authority changes the answer.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
